text.title: The pos+neg rule
text.summary: Every flow MUST carry at least one positive AND one negative case. The negative case is what catches actual production bugs.

rule.statement: Each flow MUST document at least one happy-path case AND at least one negative-path case.
rule.rationale: Positive-only coverage is a smoke test: it proves the path works under perfect conditions. Production bugs hide on imperfect paths.

section.title: Negative trumps positive (the unsexy-edge principle)
section.p: When there is time for only one extra test, write the negative one. Authentication failure, network drop, invalid input, race condition, double-submit — these are where regressions live. Happy paths get attention because they are easy to demo; negatives get attention only when something breaks at 3am.

list.item: Auth failure — wrong password, expired session, missing permission.
Network down — request times out, partial response, offline mode.
Invalid input — empty, too long, wrong type, hostile characters.
Boundary — first item, last item, page-size boundary, empty list.

antipattern.description: Flow with three positive cases and zero negatives.
antipattern.reason: Confidence theatre. Three happy paths cost the same to maintain as two happies + one negative, with a fraction of the bug-catching power.
